title: Baby Bonus Cash Gift
text:
Baby Bonus Cash Gift - SupportGoWhere
Baby Bonus Cash Gift
Share link
Help parents defray child-raising costs through regular cash payouts in their child's early years.
SCHEME HIGHLIGHTS
Baby Bonus Cash Gift enhanced by $3,000 for eligible Singaporean children
Enhancements will apply to eligible Singaporean children born on or after 14 Feb 2023
Apply online on LifeSG app/website
Collapse all sections
About this support

Eligible Singaporean children born on or after 14 Feb 2023 will qualify for the enhanced Baby Bonus Cash Gift announced at Budget 2023.

Eligible Singaporean children in the first and second birth order will receive $11,000 while children in the third and subsequent birth orders will receive $13,000.

Cash Gift will be disbursed on a regular basis, every six months until the child is six-and-a-half years old.

The Baby Bonus Cash Gift will be paid into the new Child Savings Account (CSA), which is a joint savings account that opens automatically at the same time with the Child Development Account (CDA) when a child is enrolled for the Baby Bonus Scheme. With a savings account, it will be easier for the child to receive Government payouts, like the Baby Bonus cash gift, education awards and financial assistance.

Who is this for?

Child is a Singapore Citizen

Children born on or after 14 Feb 2023

Parents are lawfully married

How to apply?

All parents can apply for Baby Bonus Scheme at LifeSG:

Bundled service together with child’s birth registration

Direct application for child who has already registered for birth

Where can I find help?

More details will be provided when available.

MSF Consolidated Hotline: 1800 111 2222

(say ‘Baby Bonus’ when asked for the hotline you want to reach)




Operating hours: Mon to Fri 8.30am to 6pm | Saturday: 8.30am to 1pm

(Closed on Public Holidays)

Airtime charges apply for mobile calls to 1800 service lines. Calls are free of charge only if made from regular land lines in Singapore.

Submit an enquiry through Ask Baby Bonus

Scheme last updated 08 Jan 2026
SUPPORT RECOMMENDER
Answer a few questions to find schemes and services you may be eligible for.
